title: Windmannia horsfieldii
intro: Windmannia horsfieldii, also known as Chinese Angelica, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae. It is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Windmannia horsfieldii include coumarins, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chinese Angelica is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicinal formulations such as soups and tonics.
taxonomy: Windmannia horsfieldii is a species within the family Strophariaceae, which belongs to the class Pezizomycetes. The Strophariaceae family is a group of fungi commonly known as the 'earth pezizas'. The class Pezizomycetes is a subclass of the larger division Ascomycota.
